name: pgvecto.rs
tagline: Postgres extension for vector similarity search, written in Rust
seoTitle: pgvecto.rs - Vector Search for PostgreSQL in Rust
metaDescription: pgvecto.rs is a Rust-based Postgres extension that adds vector similarity search with a vector data type, distance operators, and up to 65535 dimensions.
overview: pgvecto.rs is a PostgreSQL extension that adds vector similarity search to your database. It is written in Rust on top of the pgrx framework, and it gives Postgres a new vector(n) data type plus distance operators so you can store and query embeddings using ordinary SQL.

It is meant for developers who already run PostgreSQL and want to keep their embeddings next to their relational data instead of standing up a separate vector store. It supports up to 65535 dimensions, extra types such as binary vectors, FP16, and INT8, and a VBASE method that combines vector search with filters and joins.

Note that the project has been succeeded by VectorChord, which the maintainers (TensorChord) describe as having better stability and performance. New work is recommended to use VectorChord, with a migration guide provided, but pgvecto.rs remains usable as described in its README.
features: Adds a vector(n) data type to PostgreSQL, supporting up to 65535 dimensions
Three distance operators: squared Euclidean (<->), negative dot product (<#>), and cosine distance (<=>)
VBASE method for combining vector search with relational queries (TopK + filter + join)
Extra data types including binary vectors, FP16 (vecf16), and INT8 to reduce memory use
Runtime SIMD dispatch that picks instructions based on the host CPU
Stores index data separately from PostgreSQL's native storage engine

gettingStarted.intro: The quickest path is the official Docker image, which ships Postgres with the extension already built. Then enable the extension and run vector queries in plain SQL.

steps.heading: Run the Docker image
steps.body: Start a Postgres container with pgvecto.rs preinstalled. The default user is postgres and the password is the one you set here.

steps.heading: Connect and enable the extension
steps.body: Connect with psql, then create the vectors extension so the vector type and operators become available.

steps.heading: Create a table with a vector column
steps.body: Enable the extension and define a table that holds embeddings as a vector column.

steps.heading: Insert and search vectors
steps.body: Insert some embeddings, then order by a distance operator to find the nearest matches.

useCases: Add semantic search over text or image embeddings to an app that already uses PostgreSQL
Build a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) or question-answering backend that keeps embeddings alongside relational data
Run vector similarity queries with SQL filters and joins in a single Postgres query instead of a separate vector database
Reduce embedding memory footprint with FP16 (vecf16), INT8, or binary vector types on high-dimensional data
